text: Carlsbad Caverns National Park
zh: 卡尔斯巴德洞窟国家公园

text: state of New Mexico is fittingly described as the land of enchantment
zh: 新墨西哥州被恰如其分地称为“魔法之地”

text: the home of Carlsbad Caverns National Park.
zh: 这里是卡尔斯巴德洞窟国家公园的所在地。

text: High in southeastern New Mexico, within the Guadalupe Mountains, are 46 ,000 acres of wilderness.
zh: 在新墨西哥州东南部瓜达卢佩山脉深处,坐落着4.6万英亩的荒野。

text: Here, on an arid plateau, water, seeping and trickling drop by drop,
zh: 在这片干旱的高原上,水一滴一滴地渗透、滴落,

text: has over eons created lavish formations in limestone caves.
zh: 历经漫长岁月,在石灰岩洞穴中造就了繁复瑰丽的岩层形态。

text: formed by sulfuric acid dissolving the rock deep underground.
zh: 这些洞穴是由硫酸在地下深处溶蚀岩石而形成的。

text: A cave of strange, often bizarre beauty. Carlsbad Caverns.
zh: 卡尔斯巴德洞窟,是一处奇异、甚至可以说是怪诞之美的洞穴。

text: Around the end of the 19th century, the cave was still unknown to white settlers.
zh: 19世纪末,这个洞穴还不为白人定居者所知。

text: Then one day, a lone 16 -year -old cowboy, Jim White by name,
zh: 后来有一天,一个16岁的孤身牛仔,名叫吉姆·怀特,

text: saw a huge mass of of bats rising from the desert hills. He assumed rightly they'd come from a cave.
zh: 看到大群蝙蝠从沙漠山丘中飞起,他猜想它们一定是从某个洞穴中出来的,这个判断是对的。

text: Any hole in the ground which could house such a gigantic army of bats, he commented later, must be a whale of a big cave.
zh: 他后来说,能容纳如此庞大蝙蝠群的地洞,一定是个巨大无比的洞穴。

text: Jim went to see for himself. Lowering himself on a homemade made ladder he began exploring and
zh: 吉姆决定亲自去看个究竟。他顺着自制的梯子爬下去,开始了探索,

text: he kept on exploring for years to come when he told the local folk what he discovered
zh: 此后多年他一直坚持探索,当他把发现告诉当地人时,

text: they refused to believe him in time curious visitors started coming.
zh: 起初人们不肯相信他,但渐渐地,好奇的游客开始络绎而来。

text: Jim lowered those first tourists down into the cave in a big guano bucket.
zh: 吉姆用一个大蝙蝠粪桶把最早的那批游客吊入洞中。

text: Today, a modern elevator accommodates visitors,
zh: 如今,一部现代化电梯载着游客,

text: taking them 750 feet down to the cave floor.
zh: 将他们送到750英尺以下的洞穴底部。

text: This is what Jim White named the Big Room, A limestone chamber of extraordinary proportions.
zh: 这就是吉姆·怀特命名的“大房间”,一个规模宏大的石灰岩洞厅。

text: It could hold six football fields.
zh: 它足以容纳六个足球场。

text: Fantasy shapes and forms emerge from darkness.
zh: 奇幻的形状和轮廓从黑暗中浮现出来。

text: Stalagmites rising from the floor like petrified fingers.
zh: 石笋从地面拔起,宛如凝固的手指。

text: Stalactites from the shadowy ceiling, 250
zh: 钟乳石从阴影笼罩的洞顶垂下,高达250

text: feet up, biting down like gigantic dragon's teeth.
zh: 英尺,犹如巨龙的獠牙向下咬合。

text: These have been created by eons of slowly dripping water.
zh: 这些景观都是流水历经漫长岁月缓慢滴落而成的。

text: Each drop laced with calcium carbonate.
zh: 每一滴水都带着碳酸钙。

text: It can take thousands of years to add just one inch.
zh: 往往需要数千年才能增长一英寸。

text: Jim White gave many of the cavern's features the names we call them by today.
zh: 洞穴中许多景观的名字,都是吉姆·怀特当年取的,沿用至今。

text: Temple of the Sun. Painted Grotto.
zh: 太阳神殿、彩绘洞、

text: Chinese theater. Rock of ages.
zh: 中国剧场、万古磐石。

text: It is with a sense of awe that visitors explore the chambers today.
zh: 如今游客游览这些洞厅时,依然心怀敬畏。

text: As Jim discovered, parts of the cave are dense with bats.
zh: 正如吉姆当年发现的那样,洞穴的某些部分密布着蝙蝠。

text: Each summer evening, several hundred thousand of them take flight through the cave's mouth and forage for food, eating their weight in insects every night.
zh: 每到夏季的傍晚,数十万只蝙蝠从洞口飞出觅食,每晚吃掉相当于自身体重的昆虫。

text: The park has three good hiking trails, affording the visitor ample opportunity to sightsee the surrounding desert.
zh: 公园内有三条优质的徒步小径,让游客有充分机会饱览周围的沙漠风光。

text: Today, the work initiated by Jim White and his homemade ladder
zh: 如今,吉姆·怀特当年用自制梯子开创的探索工作,

text: is being carried out by geologists who are slowly penetrating the ever -increasing depths of this remarkable cave.
zh: 正由地质学家们继续进行,他们不断深入这个奇特洞穴日益幽深之处。

text: Beyond what has been explored, one can imagine what vast caverns of unknown character,
zh: 在已探明区域之外,人们可以想象,还有多少未知性质的浩瀚洞穴,

text: of of magic and mystery lie beyond.
zh: 充满魔力与神秘,等待着人们去发现。
